mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue expansion and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 4.0% establishes restrictive monetary conditions that compress equity discount rates and force negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates signals structural labor market deterioration, which reduces aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly expands corporate earnings capacity and improves equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index reading below 90.0 signals severe manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where INDPRO falls below 90.0.
